instant.evidence: Runs in the My-Day build daemon thread (myday_build_orchestrator) with store-first output and build_status.json progress writes, so the founder sees the plan land in one build pass without a stalled UI.

natural.evidence: Outputs plain-language user stories ('As a ... I want ... so that ...') with Given/When/Then acceptance for non-technical founders — no jargon, the Gherkin doubles as the demo script.

permitted.evidence: Generation is operator-initiated inside the approval-gated My-Day build flow; the only side effects are todos + documents published to the operator's OWN Basecamp via operator-scoped OAuth (basecamp_build_writer / project_plan_reconciler). It references no internal code and cannot post outside the operator's project.

adaptive.evidence: Each artifact runs a maker/checker/refiner loop: a separate checker grades against a strict rubric and the refiner fixes the named gaps, bounded by MAX_REFINE (no unbounded loop). The maker never grades itself.

contextual.evidence: Domain, users, entities, and stack are derived from the idea + discovery choices; the chain is sequential so every story cites the exact REQ-### it fulfills (traceability by construction), closing the 'tickets don't coordinate the requirements' gap.

transparent.evidence: Store-first writes REQUIREMENTS/ARCHITECTURE/BUILD_GUIDE/TRACEABILITY.md + deep_plan.json to output/{slug}/; the Requirements Traceability Matrix is published to Basecamp; each todo shows Fulfills:REQ-### + Gherkin; the deterministic gate result is logged. The full plan is inspectable.

governance.evidence: Maker/checker separation (the maker never grades itself) plus the deterministic trace gate (deep_plan_trace) that FAILS CLOSED on an orphan must-have requirement. Declared as a governed runtime entrypoint in config/tbi_runtime_agents.json (recommend_only) and runs only inside the approval-gated build pipeline.

observability.evidence: Logs each stage's checker score + pass and the trace-gate summary; writes build_status.json at each phase; emits the RTM document so coverage is visible to a reviewer.

availability.evidence: Runs as a non-blocking background daemon; store-first persists every artifact to disk before any Basecamp publish so a heavy or failed run never strands the build; the orchestrator records an error status instead of raising.

lexicon.evidence: Canonical vocabulary throughout — REQ-### requirements, STORY-### slices, dynamic r0..rN releases, Given/When/Then acceptance — consistent with docs/specs/myday-project-build-story-decomposition.md.

solid.evidence: The deterministic traceability gate and the pure helper functions are covered by first-class unit tests (tests/execution/advisory/test_deep_plan_trace.py, test_deep_plan.py, test_deep_plan_publisher.py); the gate is LLM-free and rerunnable.

layers.name: Agent-Aware Governance
layers.how: Sequential maker/checker (maker never grades itself) + a deterministic fail-closed traceability gate; the generator is a declared, recommend-only runtime entrypoint.

layers.name: Observability & Feedback
layers.how: Per-stage checker scores, the trace-gate summary, build_status.json progress, and a published RTM make the plan's quality and coverage observable.

layers.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration
layers.how: The chain is itself a maker/checker/refiner orchestration, and it designs the product's multi-agent system (bounded contexts → agents owning REQ-ids).

notes: Tier-0 runtime AI now under the TBI gate (previously uncovered). The story-driven generator replaces the parallel ticket generator; its traceability gate is deterministic and fail-closed, so the strongest trust control (every story coordinates the requirements document) is enforced by code, not by the model.
